paper_title: Assessing Successional Trends of a 2015 Lake Erie Microcystis Bloom

methods.description: Procedure for collecting environmental and biological data (pH, phytoplankton samples, specific chlorophyll a) from Lake Erie during the 2015 Microcystis bloom, supplementing with total chlorophyll a data from a previous study.

steps.description: Water column pH was recorded every 30 min via a Yellow Spring Instruments 6600v2 multiprobe sonde suspended at 1 m depth from a buoy located between South Bass and Gibraltar Islands (N 41.66 • , W 82.92 • ).
steps.searchable_summary: Record water column pH using a YSI multiprobe sonde at a specific location and depth.

steps.description: Water samples for phytoplankton community composition were collected next to the buoy several times a week.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect water samples for phytoplankton community composition analysis.

steps.description: Diatom-specific and cyanobacteria-specific chlorophyll a concentrations were recorded via a bbe Moldaenke FluoroProbe (Beutler et al., 2002).
steps.searchable_summary: Measure diatom-specific and cyanobacteria-specific chlorophyll a concentrations using a FluoroProbe.

steps.description: Total chlorophyll a concentrations corresponding to the sampling period have been provided (Supplementary Figure 2), with the complete details of this dataset found in the original publication (Chaffin et al., 2018).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain total chlorophyll a data from a previous publication/dataset.
inputs: Reference to Chaffin et al., 2018 publication
